reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer Amir Goldstein suggested that FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP_OPEN should imply skipping optional operations like lseek/ioctl/flock, and proposed a mental model where atomic open sends RELEASE to the server and uses FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP_OPEN semantics for the first open.
reviews.raw_body:
FWIW, those are all optional operations that already have
a default kernel implementation, so I suggest that OPEN passthrough
implies that we just skip them, same as you did for FLUSH.

It is worth mentioning that the atomic open code path still goes
through the server, which means that server could return
FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP_OPEN entry and either opt-in
for FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH or not and also that the server does
provide ff->fh and does expect release in this case.

Semantically this seems fine, the only thing to watch out for is
that because FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP_OPEN is for the lifetime
of the inode, it is almost as if ff->passthrough_open is redundant,
only it is not, for this first atomic open.

Maybe the mental model would be simpler if atomic open
would send RELEASE to the server and use the
FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP_OPEN semantics
(force FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH and no lseek/ioctl/flock) for this
first open as well, to be more consistent.

Thanks,
Amir.

2026-04-22.patch_summary: This patch adds a new feature to the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module called 'passthrough open'. It allows the server to indicate that certain operations should be skipped and instead performed directly on the backing file. Specifically, when the FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP_OPEN flag is set, the kernel will skip sending FUSE_OPEN/FUSE_OPENDIR requests to the server and open the backing file directly. This also implies passthrough readdir for directories and read/write operations for regular files. Additionally, if the server handles truncation logic in its FUSE_OPEN handler, the vfs layer will call handle_truncate() which may trigger a fuse_setattr() call to truncate the backing file directly or send a FUSE_SETATTR request to the server.
